name: Club of the Dagda
description: The Club of the Dagda, also known as the Lorg Mór, is a legendary weapon associated with the Dagda, a prominent god in Irish mythology. This massive club was not merely a weapon of brute force, but a magical artifact capable of both destruction and restoration. One end of the club could kill with a single blow, while the other could bring the slain back to life. This duality reflects the Dagda's role as a figure of both power and benevolence, a chieftain who controlled life and death. The club's immense size and weight required extraordinary strength to wield, further emphasizing the Dagda's divine status. Its creation is not explicitly detailed in many texts, but it's understood to be an ancient artifact, possibly crafted by the Fomorians or by the Dagda himself. The club's significance lies in its representation of the Dagda's multifaceted nature and his absolute control over the forces of the world. Legends say that the Dagda used the club to defend his people, punish wrongdoers, and even stir his cauldron of plenty. The club is a symbol of his authority, wisdom, and the balance between destruction and creation that he embodied.
subtitle: Weapon of Life and Death
origin: Likely crafted by the Dagda or Fomorians

keyMyths.title: Dagda's Club and the Fomorians
keyMyths.description: The Dagda used his club to defeat Fomorian invaders, demonstrating its power and his role as protector. One blow could slay enemies, while another could revive his allies, turning the tide of battle.

keyMyths.title: The Club and the Cauldron
keyMyths.description: Some stories suggest the Dagda used his club to stir his magical cauldron of plenty. This highlights the club's association with both destruction and the provision of sustenance.

sources.author: Peter Berresford Ellis
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of Celtic myths and deities, including the Dagda and his artifacts.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of Celtic myths and deities, including the Dagda and his artifacts.

sources.author: Lady Gregory
sources.description: A collection of Irish myths and legends, offering insights into the Dagda's role and the power of his club.
